assets.prompt: luminous bright pastel mystical sky background hand-painted watercolor, soft sky-wash blue gradient filling most of frame, NOT dark NOT black NOT night, fluffy mushroom-cream cumulus clouds drifting in upper third, very faint distant moss-sage rolling hill silhouettes barely visible at horizon line bottom edge, gentle saffron-glow warm sun haze backlit from upper-right corner, atmospheric daylight haze, watercolor wet-edge bleeds, paper grain texture, soft Studio Ghibli sky illustration, NOT detailed foreground, soft focus, NO mountains NO ink-aubergine NO dark masses NO characters NO trees NO emoji NO photoreal, palette mushroom-cream sky-wash-blue moss-sage saffron-glow only, predominantly blue and cream values

assets.prompt: alien mushroom forest mid-distance silhouettes hand-painted watercolor, translucent moss-sage canopy mushrooms in mid-plane, mushroom-cream tree trunks scattered, faded-rose lichen patches, sky-wash-blue mist drifting between trees, gentle ground line at bottom 1/4 of image, transparent upper half showing sky beyond, paper grain wet-edge bleeds, ink underdrawing, Studio Ghibli x Moebius, NO characters NO foreground close-up flora NO emoji NO photoreal, palette moss-sage mushroom-cream faded-rose sky-wash-blue ink-aubergine
assets.notes: On-style first try. Built-in sky-window in upper portion makes it stack-compatible without true alpha.
BiRefNet stripped trees/ground/lichen/mist; only kept 5-6 largest mushroom caps. Not a useful mid-layer replacement.

assets.prompt: foreground frame elements of mushroom forest hand-painted watercolor, large ink-aubergine drooping vines and mushroom-stalks at the edges of the image (mostly left and right edges and bottom), forest-deep silhouette of large foliage, faded-rose drippy berries hanging in upper corners, ink-aubergine darker edge shadows for depth-framing effect, transparent center showing through to mid/far layers, paper grain heavy outline, Moebius Arzach edge linework, NO characters NO sky NO ground center NO emoji NO photoreal, palette ink-aubergine forest-deep faded-rose mushroom-cream
assets.notes: Excellent depth-frame composition. Engine should apply multiply blend mode (canvas globalCompositeOperation='multiply') so light center disappears against bright far/mid layers.
BiRefNet kept only the largest mushrooms; lost vines, berries, and frame foliage.

skipped.reason: BiRefNet wiped image entirely (2.4KB pure white). Cave scenes have no clear foreground subject. File deleted.

engine_recommendations.alternative_for_near: If multiply blend introduces unwanted darkening, run a one-shot Pillow color-key script (PIL.Image: convert near-white pixels to alpha=0) instead of BiRefNet. Cheaper and more controllable for landscape framing layers.

gotchas: BiRefNet 400 polling error: response_url returns 400 while job is still IN_QUEUE; fix is to poll status_url instead and only fetch response_url after status==COMPLETED

tile-trap (memory-confirmed): square+STEM keywords give full landscapes for tile prompts; tile-wall-cracked v1 and v2 both rendered full scenes. v3 worked by DROPPING the STEM entirely and using a stripped close-up macro prompt with stacked anti-landscape negatives

enemy-spark v1 rendered as a furry creature in a forest because the prompt described it as a "creature with NOT eyes NOT face" — model still grew a body. v2 fixed by leading with "ONE tiny electric energy spark-ball game hazard, NOT a creature NOT a body NOT fur"

enemy-suctioncrawler SUCCESS: image-only Flux Dev rendered actual disc-shaped suction-cup pads on all four limbs in one shot. Cosmo-DNA bridge in description ("matching reference Cosmo hand-style") + "BIG ROUND DISC pads NOT fingers NOT claws" worked despite memory note that suction-cups are deterministically un-renderable. The difference: this is a 4-legged creature on the ground (sample bias for arthropod legs ending in foot-pads), not a biped where the model fights to render fingers

sprints.sprint: Sprint 7A — Multi-frame Cosmo via Flux Control LoRA Canny
sprints.strategy: Programmatic stick-figure skeletons (PIL) → Flux Control LoRA Canny → BiRefNet
sprints.goal: 6 distinct pose-textures wired to Cosmo state-machine (idle/run/jump/fall/cling/damage)
pipeline: 1. Generate 6 stick-figure skeletons in Python/PIL with thick black lines + BIG black discs at hand-tips (signature suction-cup constraint)
2. Upload all 6 skeletons + canonical to fal.ai storage

3. For each pose: fal-ai/flux-control-lora-canny with skeleton as control_lora_image_url, NO image_url (canonical drags pose back to neutral — Sprint 5B learning), control_lora_strength=1.2, style-first prompt
5. Wire textures in L1Scene.preload + Cosmo.updateAnim() texture-swap state machine

knees-tucked apex-of-jump, arms reaching up with discs above head
T-pose falling, arms outstretched horizontally with discs at tips
side-view, both suction-cups extending right toward virtual wall
production_assets.fidelity: 9/10 character, 9/10 pose. setFlipX in updateAnim for left-wall mirror

production_assets.pose: knockback, arms thrown out + slightly back, legs splayed, faded-rose blush spots bonus

case_study_artifacts: raw-walk-1.png (v1, with canonical image_url — FAILED, pose lost, kawaii eyes, tail back)
raw-walk-1-v2.png (skeleton-only, strength 1.2 — pose held but anemic line-art style)
raw-walk-1-v3.png / raw-walk-1-FINAL.png (skeleton-only, strength 1.2, style-first prompt — PRODUCTION)
scripts: scripts/sprint7a/cosmo_controlnet.py — fal.ai pipeline (modes: test, test2, batch, all, redo-walk2)
wiring_changes: src/phaser/scenes/L1Scene.ts:156 — Cosmo init texture changed to 'cosmo-walk-1'
src/phaser/scenes/L1Scene.ts — removed redundant swapCosmoTexture() (Cosmo.updateAnim handles it)
src/phaser/entities/Cosmo.ts — replaced playStateAnim with updateAnim() texture-swap state machine
src/phaser/entities/Cosmo.ts — added walkPhase + walkFrameToggle for ~133ms walk-cycle alternation
key_learnings: Flux Control LoRA Canny WITHOUT image_url + skeleton-only is THE working pose-control pipeline. Passing canonical as image_url drags the result back to canonical neutral-stand pose — confirms Sprint 5B finding that image-to-image is a noise-init not skeletal-anchor

control_lora_strength sweet spot is 1.2 — at 0.85 the skeleton barely matters, at 1.0 the prompt-style wins and pose collapses to A-pose, at 1.2 pose holds and prompt still gets enough weight for character DNA
Programmatic stick-figures with BIG black discs at hand-tips force the Flux model to render JET-BLACK suction-cup-pads — it's not a sample-bias problem, it's a CONTROL problem solved by canny edge
Style-first prompt ordering (Studio Ghibli watercolor cues FIRST, character DNA SECOND) preserves more painterly look at high control_lora_strength
Inter-frame eye-style consistency is fragile (some frames get 1 ink-eye, others get 2 black-disc-eyes) — at 120px in-game this is acceptable, but a per-frame seed-lock could improve
Queue wait times for control-lora-canny can hit 100-150s during peak — bump submit_and_poll deadline to 600s
open_issues: Inter-frame style/eye drift: walk-1 has 1 ink-eye, walk-2 has 2 black-disc-eyes, hurt has 1 ink-eye + blush spots. Not catastrophic but visible if you compare side-by-side. A future pass could fix walk-2 with a different seed

Cling-right uses setFlipX for left-wall cling — works but the suction-cups will appear on the right side of the body when flipped (since body is asymmetric). Acceptable since both arms reach toward the wall in the source pose
Hurt-frame is reused for death state with no rotation — if death looks weird in playtesting, add a Z-rotation tween in Cosmo.takeDamage()
